title: Physics Truth Stack
summary: Physics is the study of matter, energy, motion, fields, and the deep rules that govern the observable universe.
starterPack: Start with the Feynman Lectures for fundamentals.
Use NIST and APS for authoritative references.
Watch PBS Space Time for intuition and big-picture context.

tiers.title: First Principles
tiers.description: The core laws and habits of thought that make physics coherent.

items.name: Conservation laws
items.description: Energy, momentum, and charge are conserved in closed systems.

items.name: Measurement and falsifiability
items.description: A good physical model must be testable against reality.

items.name: Scale changes behaviour
items.description: Different regimes can require different theories or approximations.

tiers.title: Primary Authorities
tiers.description: Standards, journals, and labs closest to the source of truth.

items.name: National Institute of Standards and Technology
items.description: Measurement standards, constants, and precision science.

items.name: CERN
items.description: Large-scale experimental particle physics.

items.name: American Physical Society
items.description: Major professional body and publication ecosystem.

tiers.title: Mature Interpreters
tiers.description: People who explain physics with discipline and judgement.

items.name: Richard Feynman
items.description: Brilliant foundational clarity and physical intuition.

items.name: Sean Carroll
items.description: Careful public explanation of cosmology and fundamental physics.

items.name: Sabine Hossenfelder
items.description: Candid critique of physics culture and claims.

tiers.title: Practitioners
tiers.description: The people turning physics into real-world work.

items.name: Experimental physicists
items.description: Design experiments and extract signals from noisy data.

items.name: Applied physicists
items.description: Work on sensors, imaging, materials, and devices.

items.name: Metrology specialists
items.description: Maintain the standards that underpin exact measurement.

tiers.title: Institutions
tiers.description: The homes where serious physics is taught, tested, and scaled.

items.name: National labs
items.description: Large-scale experimental and applied physics capability.

items.name: University physics departments
items.description: Core research and training institutions.

items.name: Standards bodies
items.description: Formal homes of physical measurement standards.

tiers.title: Media and Publications
tiers.description: Trusted publications that translate current physics work well.

items.name: Quanta Magazine
items.description: High-quality science journalism with strong physics coverage.

items.name: Physics Today
items.description: Professional overview of developments in the field.

items.name: Nature Physics
items.description: Research and perspective from a leading journal.

tiers.title: Channels and Documentaries
tiers.description: Useful video channels for building intuition without losing rigor.

items.name: PBS Space Time
items.description: Conceptual physics and cosmology explanations.

items.name: Veritasium
items.description: Clear demonstrations and big-picture scientific thinking.

items.name: Sixty Symbols
items.description: University-backed physics explanations.

tiers.title: Frontier Watchlist
tiers.description: Where the field is moving next.

items.name: Quantum information
items.description: Information processing at quantum scale.

items.name: Gravitational wave astronomy
items.description: New ways of observing the universe.

items.name: Fusion and plasma physics
items.description: Long-horizon energy and confinement research.
